title: scorzonera circumflexa subsp. glaberrima
intro: Scorzonera circumflexa subsp. glaberrima is a subspecies of Scorzonera, a type of plant that belongs to the sunflower family. It is also known as the "viper's grass" due to its potentially toxic properties. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating gastrointestinal issues. Scorzonera circumflexa subsp. glaberrima can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
